query:
INSERT INTO public.vocabulary_words (word, definition, example_sentence, part_of_speech, difficulty, exam_type)
VALUES
  ('Quarter', 'To divide into four parts', 'The author hoped the new policy would quarter tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Quarto', 'A book made up of four leaves, or eight pages', 'The essay highlights the quarto of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quasi', 'Resembling or having a likeness to something', 'Over time, the community learned to quasi its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quasi-', '1 english', 'Her quasi- became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quaver', 'To shake in a trembling manner', 'Critics argued that the plan might quaver the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quay', 'A landing place for boats along the edge of a body of water', 'The essay highlights the quay of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Querulous', 'Complaining in a petulant or whining manner', 'The reviewer described the tone as querulous, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Query', 'To ask, inquire', 'Over time, the community learned to query its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quibble', 'To complain or argue in a trivial or petty manner', 'The author hoped the new policy would quibble tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quiescence', 'The state of being at rest', 'The essay highlights the quiescence of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quiescent', 'In a state of inactivity or dormancy', 'The reviewer described the tone as quiescent, which shaped how readers interpreted the claim.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quietus', 'A finishing stroke', 'Scholars noted a striking quietus between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quintessence', 'A thing that is the most perfect example of its type', 'The essay highlights the quintessence of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quintessential', 'Representing the most typical or ideal example', '“Shakespeare is considered the quintessential English playwright.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quintet', '(music) a composition in five parts', 'Her quintet became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quirk', 'Something unusual about someone''s manner or style', 'Her quirk became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quite', 'Wholly', 'Scholars noted a striking quite between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quixotic', 'Unrealistically idealistic', '“His quixotic plan to end world hunger lacked practical solutions.”', 'adjective', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quizzical', 'Odd, comical', 'Her quizzical became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quorum', 'The number of members of something required to do business', 'Critics argued that the plan might quorum the very problem it aimed to solve.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Quotidian', 'Daily', 'The essay highlights the quotidian of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rabid', 'Irrationally extreme in opinion or practice', 'Her rabid became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Raconteur', 'A person who is skilled in relating stories and anecdotes', 'Scholars noted a striking raconteur between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Racy', 'Slightly improper or indelicate', 'Scholars noted a striking racy between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),

  ('Radiate', 'To extend, send or spread out from a center', 'The author hoped the new policy would radiate tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Radical', 'Favouring change at the root cause of a matter', 'The essay highlights the radical of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Radix', 'A number taken as the base of a system of numbers', 'Scholars noted a striking radix between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Raffish', 'Mildly or sometimes engagingly disreputable or nonconformist', 'The essay highlights the raffish of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rail', 'To complain violently (against, about)', 'Over time, the community learned to rail its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Raillery', 'Good-natured ridicule, jest or banter', 'Scholars noted a striking raillery between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Raiment', 'Clothing, garments, dress, material', 'What seemed raiment at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rambl', 'Disorganized and lengthy', 'Scholars noted a striking rambl between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ramble', 'To move about aimlessly, or on a winding course', 'The author hoped the new policy would ramble tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ramification', 'A related problem or consequence', 'The essay highlights the ramification of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'hard'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ramify', 'To divide or spread out into branches or branchlike parts', 'Over time, the community learned to ramify its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Ramose', 'Having branches, branching', 'What seemed ramose at first proved central to the author''s argument.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rampant', 'Violent in action or spirit', 'Scholars noted a striking rampant between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rampart', 'A broad elevation used as fortification around a place', 'Her rampart became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rancid', 'Being rank in taste or smell', 'Scholars noted a striking rancid between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rancor', 'Long-standing bitterness or resentment', 'Her rancor became evident only after reading the full passage.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rancorous', 'Characterized by bitterness or resentment', 'The passage presents a rancorous portrait of life during the transition.', 'adjective', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rankle', 'To cause irritation or deep bitterness', 'Over time, the community learned to rankle its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rant', 'To speak or shout at length in an uncontrollable anger', 'The author hoped the new policy would rant tensions in the region.', 'verb', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rapacious', 'Given to seizing for plunder or the satisfaction of greed', 'Over time, the community learned to rapacious its differences through dialogue.', 'verb', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rapacity', 'The quality of being rapacious, voracity', 'The essay highlights the rapacity of the reform as a turning point in the debate.', 'noun', 'medium'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type),
  ('Rapid', 'Describes a process or concept which occurs quickly', 'Scholars noted a striking rapid between the two historical accounts.', 'noun', 'easy'::public.difficulty, 'SAT'::public.exam_type)
ON CONFLICT (word, exam_type) DO UPDATE SET
  definition = EXCLUDED.definition,
  example_sentence = EXCLUDED.example_sentence,
  part_of_speech = EXCLUDED.part_of_speech,
  difficulty = EXCLUDED.difficulty;
